accept: A documented bootstrap snippet exists (likely in act help or README) for getting act into a fresh repo from CC Web in one or two commands.
The snippet is tested end-to-end against a freshly-cloned target repo in a CC Web environment (or a reasonable local simulation).
.mcp.json template that other repos can copy is provided.
payload.description: act is currently usable only by building the binary from source ('go build -o bin/act ./cmd/act'). For other repos to dogfood act in CC Web sessions, the install path needs to be one of: (a) a published GitHub Release binary the session downloads ('gh release download v0.1.0 --pattern act-linux-amd64'); (b) 'go install github.com/aac/act/cmd/act@latest' (requires public Go module access); (c) a brew tap ('brew install aac/act/act'). CC Web sessions run in a Linux container with go and gh available, so (a) and (b) should both work. Decide on the canonical bootstrap snippet that goes in a target repo's .mcp.json and CLAUDE.md so any agent in any CC Web session can install + use act in <30s.
payload.title: make act available in Claude Code Web sessions
